//2: ErrorBoundary.tsx is excluded as unreachable-by-design: expo-router wraps every route in its own error boundary, so route render crashes never propagate to this component (verified by intercepting a recipe payload that guarantees a React child-type crash — expo-router recovered to home). It only guards provider/layout-level failures, which no browser flow can trigger. Making the branded fallback actually reachable requires exporting route-level ErrorBoundary adapters (tracked as a Backlog story).
//: E2E coverage scope policy: coverage is measured over everything chromium E2E can actually execute. Native-only branches (Platform.OS !== 'web': Alert.alert, SecureStore) are excluded at the source level via '/* istanbul ignore */' comments with per-site justification — they are 100% unit-tested instead. The thresholds below are a RATCHET toward the 100% goal (Notion: 'Goal: raise E2E coverage to 100%'): raise them as gaps close (household, profile, cook mode, notify() web path, client.ts error paths, ErrorBoundary); never lower them.
